title: Weekend Box Office: 'Abigail' Flops as 'Civil War' Stays on Top
text: Universal's horror film Abigail disappointed audiences with a weak $10.1 million opening, allowing Civil War to retain the #1 position despite a significant 56% drop to $11.2 million in its second weekend. The article notes that Abigail represents one of seven horror releases in eight weeks, yet it fell short compared to previous genre successes. The film received a B CinemaScore, which the writer considers respectable for horror, but it apparently lacked distinguishing qualities amid similar recent releases. The overall box office performance was anemic, with the weekend barely reaching $66 million. The author emphasizes this represents a concerning trend: five sub-$70 million totals out of 16 weekends in 2024, contrasting sharply with 2019 when none occurred despite lower ticket prices. Year-to-date box office performance has suffered significantly, down approximately 19% compared to the previous year. April specifically has been challenging despite some successful releases, with franchise films from March carrying much of the box office burden. Two other new releases—The Ministry of Ungentlemanly Warfare ($9 million) and the anime Spy x Family Code: White (nearly $5 million)—also underperformed expectations, failing to meaningfully boost weekend totals.
